meta.note: Corridors as first-class risk entities. p0 = reasoned 30-day base rate prior; signals = BAKED snapshot of the mid-2026 posture (news/ais/sanctions/market each 0..1, normalized). Live mode (RA5) replaces the snapshot; the fusion math is identical.

news.note: log-odds per unit signal; news leads physical events (GDELT literature)

ais.note: behavioral signal: dark ships / reroutes / transit deviation

corridors.name: Strait of Hormuz
corridors.p0_basis: reasoned prior: persistent threat, never closed in 40 years of incidents (Tanker War, 2012 threats, 2019 seizures)
corridors.signals_basis: baked snapshot: IRGC drills active (news rail), war-risk premiums rising, moderate shadow-fleet transit

corridors.name: Bab el-Mandeb
corridors.p0_basis: reasoned prior: active attack era since 2023 (Red Sea crisis)
corridors.signals_basis: baked snapshot: convoy escorts extended, transits ~-40% vs baseline, freight premiums elevated

corridors.name: Suez Canal
corridors.p0_basis: reasoned prior: downstream of Bab el-Mandeb pressure + 2021 grounding precedent
corridors.signals_basis: baked snapshot: southbound convoy volumes down ~18% (news rail)

corridors.name: Strait of Malacca
corridors.p0_basis: reasoned prior: piracy incidents, no state-level closure threat

corridors.name: Cape of Good Hope
corridors.p0_basis: reasoned prior: open-ocean route, weather risk only — it is the fallback, not a chokepoint
corridors.signals_basis: baked snapshot: elevated traffic from Red Sea reroutes (congestion, not threat)
